rulings.Case Title: LISA KROGER EITANI VS. ADI SIMAYOF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Pltf'S Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records, Or In The Alternative To Modify
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 2, 2013, Line 2: Defendant SIMUS, INC., a California corporation, dba, WORLD OF CHARMS INC.'S Motion to Quash Plaintiff's Deposition Subpoenas for Production of Business Records or, in the Alternative, to Modify Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to discuss their positions on whether, under the holding of Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568, 1586-1587, the court may hear the motion to quash given that the discovery motion cutoff date has passed and trial is set to begin 13 days from the date of the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ZF MICRO SOLUTIONS INC. et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Resposnes To Discovery, Production Of Documents, To Establish Admissions And For Sanctions Against Pltfs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 2, 2013, Line 5: Defendants SUSAN HANDELMAN'S and ROPERS, MAJESKI, KOHN & BENTLEY PC'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, Production Of Documents, To Establish Admissions And For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED with respect to Defendants' motion to compel production of documents; within 15 days Plaintiffs David Feldman and ZF Micro Solutions, Inc. shall produce documents responsive to Defendants' requests for production. The remainder of the relief requested is DENIED AS MOOT WITHOUT PREJUDICE to Defendants' ability to move to compel David Feldman and ZF Micro Solutions to provide further responses to Defendants' Special Interrogatories, Set One; Form Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Admissions set one. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE MARTHE VS. THAI PARODI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Thia Parodi'S Further Responses To Discovery, Set One, And Req. For Attorney'S Fees And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 2, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff NICOLE MARTHE'S Motion To Compel Thia Parodi's Further Responses To Discovery, Set One, And Requests For Attorney's Fees And Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff's motion is DENIED with respect to Form Interrogatories Nos. 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1, 16.1, 16.2, 16.3, 16.4, 16.5, 16.6, 16.9, 16.10. Defendant's has provided adequate factual responses to the interrogatories. Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED with respect to Form Interrogatories 15.1 and 20.8. Defendant shall provide code-compliant supplemental responses to the interrogatories within 15 days. Plaintiff's motion is DENIED with respect to Requests for Production 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 15. Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED with respect to Requests for Production Nos. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10 & 14. Within 15 days Defendant shall serve code-compliant verified responses and produce documents responsive to the requests. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH J. RADER JR VS. SUN LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF CANADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Pltff'S Notices Of Taking Deposition Of Sun Life'S Person Most Knowledgeable
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 2, 2013, Line 4: Defendant SUN LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF CANADA'S Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Notices Of Taking Deposition Of Sun Life's Person Most Knowledgeable Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The parties are to meet and confer prior to the hearing to agree on as many issues as they can concerning the scope of the person most knowledgeable deposition notices and shall report to the court any areas of disagreement remaining at the hearing. With respect to the location of the depositions,Code of Civil Procedure Section 1989 does not preclude the entity deposition from taking place in San Francisco. Code of Civil Procedure Section 1989, on its face, applies to natural persons. Given that entities themselves are not witnesses, but rather speak through the agents they designate pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 2023.230, Section 1989 does not limit the Court's ability to order an entity party to produce its agents at a location specified in Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.250(d). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH J. RADER JR VS. SUN LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF CANADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Mtn To Quash Pltf'S Notice Of Taking Depo If Sun Life'S Person Most Knowledgeable
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 2, 2013, Line 4: Defendant SUN LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF CANADA'S Amended Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Notice Of Taking Deposition Of Sun Life's Person Most Knowledgeable Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The parties are to meet and confer prior to the hearing to agree on as many issues as they can concerning the scope of the person most knowledgeable deposition notices and shall report to the court any areas of disagreement remaining at the hearing. With respect to the location of the depositions,Code of Civil Procedure Section 1989 does not preclude the entity deposition from taking place in San Francisco. Code of Civil Procedure Section 1989, on its face, applies to natural persons. Given that entities themselves are not witnesses, but rather speak through the agents they designate pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 2023.230, Section 1989 does not limit the Court's ability to order an entity party to produce its agents at a location specified in Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.250(d). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Second Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, October 2, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CALVI MOTION FOR LEAVE TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT Continued to October 25, 2013, to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the motion to Department 302 and a copy of the proof of service, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than October 10, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN STARKEY VS. WESTERN DENTAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, October 2, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Grant. No opposition filed. The Complaint is dismissed with prejudice pursuant to Civil Procedure Code Section 581(f)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: WING K. TSE et al VS. ANITA TSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, October 2, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT ANITA TSE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action for intentional interference with expected inheritance is sustained without leave to amend. The Court grants Defendants' request for judicial notice. Because Plaintiffs have an adequate remedy in probate and have not been deprived of access to the probate system, they cannot pursue a cause of action for intentional interference with expected inheritance. (Beckwith v. Dahl (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1039, 1052, 1056) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
